data.title: DJIA Rises 235.92 Points (0.57%)
Italian 10Y Bond Yield Steadies at 8-Month High
data.description:
Italy’s 10-year government bond yield surged to 3.95%, its highest level since July 2024, fueled by growing optimism about Europe’s economic outlook as Germany’s parliament continues negotiations to expand state borrowing and approve a special infrastructure investment plan.

Meanwhile, money markets have tempered expectations for further ECB rate cuts after policymakers delivered a widely anticipated reduction in March but struck a more cautious tone on future moves while raising near-term inflation forecasts.

ECB President Christine Lagarde warned that abrupt shifts in global trade and changes to the region’s defense framework will make it increasingly difficult to maintain inflation stability.

data.title: TSX opens higher ahead of BoC's rate decision
data.description:
Canada's main stock index opened higher on Wednesday, ahead of the Bank of Canada's interest rate decision that is likely to result in a quarter-point cut.

At 9:31 a.m. ET (1331 GMT), the Toronto Stock Exchange's S&P/TSX composite index was up 0.4% at 24,342.46.

data.title: Visionary Signs Deal With Pegasus to Build 600 Battery Swap Stations in Hong Kong by 2029; Shares Up
data.description:
Visionary said Wednesday it has signed a cooperation agreement with Pegasus International Group under which Visionary will invest 420 million Hong Kong dollars ($54.1 million) to construct 600 battery swap/charging stations in Hong Kong by 2029.

The company said it will build 10 stations this year and that the construction of the first new energy vehicle battery swap station in the country will be launched this month.

Visionary said it will integrate its equipment, technology, and operating funds with Pegasus' in-site resources and other aspects to jointly explore Hong Kong's new energy vehicle battery swap/charging service market.

GV shares were up 42% in early trading.

data.title: Groupon Shares Jump After 4Q Revenue Beat, North America Recovery
data.description:
By Sabela Ojea

Shares of Groupon on Wednesday climbed after the company posted higher-than-expected revenue in the fourth quarter and added active customers in its core market, North America.

The stock was up 24% to $12.13 in pre-market trading, which would be its largest percentage increase since August 2023. Through Tuesday's close, shares have fallen 46% over the past 12 months.

The online coupon provider posted fourth-quarter revenue of $130.4 million, down 5% from the same period a year earlier but beating Wall Street expectations of $127.7 million, according to FactSet.

The company said that its active customer base stood at 15.4 million as of Dec. 31, remaining flat sequentially, but that North America active customers rose 1% to 10.3 million.

Groupon expects revenue in the first quarter to be $114 million to $117 million, compared with the $116 million expected by analysts, according to FactSet.

The company said its results respond to its business model shift, aimed at focusing on more curated experiences instead of chasing volume.

"After a bumpy third quarter, we rebounded nicely in the fourth quarter, with North America local billings growing 8%," Chief Executive Dusan Senkypl said. "We enter 2025 with momentum and a stronger foundation to accelerate growth."

Write to Sabela Ojea at sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix

data.title: MagnaChip Semiconductor Corp reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• MagnaChip Semiconductor Corp reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 7 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of -4 cents. The mean expectation of three analysts for the quarter was for a loss of 19 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from -22 cents to -13 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 24% to $63.04 million from a year ago; analysts expected $61.57 million.

  • MagnaChip Semiconductor Corp's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of 44 cents​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of $16.28 million.

  • MagnaChip Semiconductor Corp shares had risen by 0.5% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," 1 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The average consensus recommendation for the semiconductors peer group is also "buy"

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for MagnaChip Semiconductor Corp is $7.00

data.title: Ibotta's Board Approves Up to $100 Million Expansion in Stock Buyback Plan
data.description:
Ibotta said Wednesday the board has approved an up to $100 million increase in its stock buyback plan.

The company said the program has no expiration date, and repurchases may be made through open market transactions or private deals, subject to market conditions and legal requirements.

Ibotta's shares were up over 4% in recent Wednesday trading.

data.title: Trump's Trade War Gamble--Stock Market Gains Wiped Out as Canada Hits Back
data.description:
Markets took a hit as President Donald Trump threw investors into a frenzy with his latest tariff threatonly to backpedal hours later. Trump initially vowed to double tariffs on Canadian steel and aluminum to 50% after Ontario imposed a 25% electricity surcharge on U.S. exports. But when Ontario backed down, so did Trump, keeping tariffs at 25% instead. Meanwhile, Canada isn't budgingPrime Minister Mark Carney made it clear that retaliatory tariffs on $30 billion worth of U.S. goods are staying put.

The business world is feeling the heat. Goldman Sachs slashed its U.S. growth forecast to 1.7%, warning that Trump's tariffs are fueling recession risks. Harvard economist Larry Summers puts the odds of an economic downturn at 50-50. And small businesses? They're scrambling. New Jersey-based Icarus Brewing, for example, is stockpiling cans and raw materials, bracing for unpredictable price swings. Trump, however, is holding the line, insisting tariffs will force companies to move production back to U.S. soil. But with supply chains in chaos and manufacturers facing rising costs, investors are left questioning whether this protectionist push is a long-term winor a short-term disaster.

And this isn't just about Canada. Trump is already hitting Mexico and China with fresh levies and has his sights set on Europe, South Korea, and even pharmaceutical drugs and semiconductors. The market's verdict? Not great. The S&P 500's (SPY) post-election gains have evaporated, and with fears of a slowdown mounting, investors now have a new reality to grapple with: Trump's trade war isn't overit's just getting started
